library_preparation_preflight.summary: The artifact is standalone OpenTelemetry library instrumentation for Spring Web MVC 5.3, but its POM only brings OpenTelemetry APIs. Its public API and implementation reference Spring WebMVC and javax.servlet types that are expected to be supplied by the host application, so generated tests need those host/test dependencies to reach meaningful coverage.

deterministic_setup.reason: The library documentation says spring-webmvc is required to instrument Spring WebMVC, and the artifact references Spring MVC classes such as OncePerRequestFilter, DispatcherServlet, HandlerMapping, and ServletRequestPathUtils while not declaring Spring as a transitive dependency.

The artifact exposes and implements javax.servlet Filter, HttpServletRequest, HttpServletResponse, FilterChain, and related Servlet API types, but neither the artifact nor Spring WebMVC brings a servlet container API transitively.

Spring test mocks provide MockHttpServletRequest, MockHttpServletResponse, and related web test fixtures so tests can exercise the telemetry servlet filter without requiring a real servlet container or Docker image.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Use Spring Framework 5.3 / javax.servlet APIs, not Spring 6 / jakarta.servlet. Meaningful tests should create SpringWebMvcTelemetry with an OpenTelemetry instance, call createServletFilter(), and drive the returned Filter with Spring mock servlet requests/responses and a FilterChain; optionally register a small DispatcherServlet/WebApplicationContext if testing http.route extraction.
risks: Using Spring 6 or jakarta.servlet will not match this artifact, which is built for Spring WebMVC 5.3 and javax.servlet.
A pure no-op OpenTelemetry instance can exercise code paths, but span assertions would require additional OpenTelemetry SDK/testing dependencies if the generated test chooses to verify emitted spans.
